_comment: TEMPLATE — filenames starting with _ are ignored by the loader and never render. Copy this shape for real cases. STRICT SCOPE: litigation only — court cases and formal legal disputes involving data centers or large crypto-mining facilities. Every entry needs a caption, a plain-English summary in our OWN words, a known category + status, and a public sourceUrl. Describe only what the public filing ASSERTS — never characterize the merits, predict an outcome, or call any party liable before a court has ruled. Never republish filings; summarize and link out. category = nuisance | water | zoning | environmental | utility. status = filed | answered | discovery | motions | trial | judgment | appeal | resolved (active) OR settled | dismissed | nonsuited (terminal). Set national:true for out-of-Texas landmark cases. Optional stage/stageDates/stageNote override the status-derived lifecycle position (stage keys: filed, answer, discovery, motions, trial, judgment, appeal, resolved).
caption: EXAMPLE — Smith v. Example Data Centers, LLC (delete me)
plaintiffs: Short description of who is suing
defendants: Short description of who is sued
operator: The data-center or crypto operator involved
summary: One short paragraph, in our own words, describing what the filing asserts the case is about.
whyItMatters: One or two factual sentences on why it matters to Texas landowners.
stageNote: Optional one-line caption on where it stands.
